html:
[627]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 心动 - Means 'heart moved' or 'heart stirred,' indicating a sudden emotional response.
  • 密从帐外听之 - Means 'secretly listening from outside the tent,' describing a covert action.
  • 可封有用郎君 - Means 'he can be sealed as a useful young man,' where '封' (feng) is a metaphorical term for bestowing a title or honor.
  • 奴具虽健，小蛇耳 - Means 'Although my tools are strong, I am just a small snake,' where '奴具' (nu ju) refers to one's abilities or skills, and '小蛇' (xiao she) is a humble self-reference.
  • 自不敌可汗真龙 - Means 'I cannot match the true dragon of the Khan,' where '真龙' (zhen long) symbolizes the Khan's supreme power and authority.
  • 小猛蛇却赛真懒龙 - Means 'A small fierce snake can still match the lazy true dragon,' where '赛' (sai) means 'to match' or 'to compete with.'
  • 但闻惺惺若小儿梦中啼而已 - Means 'Only hearing the faint sounds as if a child is crying in a dream,' where '惺惺' (xing xing) describes a faint, indistinct sound.
  • 揭帐 - Means 'to lift the curtain,' indicating a gesture of revealing something.
  • 惟一醉不起 - Means 'Weyi is drunk and won't get up,' where '醉' (zui) means 'drunk.'
  • 持出夸示同官 - Means 'took it out to show off to his colleagues,' where '夸示' (kua shi) means 'to show off' or 'to display proudly.'
  • 手夺其词 - Means 'took the poem by hand,' where '手夺' (shou duo) means 'to take by hand.'
  • 使妇清子问登 - Means 'to have a woman named Qingzi ask Deng,' where '使' (shi) means 'to make' or 'to have.'
  • 乘暇泣谏 - Means 'took the opportunity to weep and advise,' where '乘暇' (cheng xia) means 'to take advantage of a moment' and '泣谏' (qi jian) means 'to weep and advise.'
  • 安免株坐 - Means 'how can one avoid being implicated,' where '株坐' (zhu zuo) means 'to be implicated by association.'
  • 乞为转奏 - Means 'begging to be relayed to the emperor,' where '乞' (qi) means 'to beg' and '转奏' (zhuan zou) means 'to relay to the emperor.'
  • 以正刑诛 - Means 'to correct the punishment,' where '正刑诛' (zheng xing zhu) means 'to apply the proper punishment.'
  • 寡妻匹妇 - Means 'widows and single women,' where '匹妇' (pi fu) refers to a single woman.
  • 莫不刑（形）于于 - Means 'none are exempt from being shaped,' where '刑' (xing) means 'to shape' or 'to mold.'
  • 宫帐深密 - Means 'the inner palace is deep and secluded,' where '深密' (shen mi) describes the privacy and seclusion of the palace.
  • 异言 - Means 'strange words' or 'unusual talk,' referring to inappropriate or scandalous speech.
  • 良非渺小 - Means 'it is not insignificant,' where '良' (liang) means 'indeed' or 'truly.'
  • 隐讳 - Means 'to hide or conceal,' where '隐讳' (yin hui) refers to withholding information.
  • 辄据词 - Means 'immediately based on the words,' where '辄' (zhe) means 'immediately' and '据词' (ju ci) means 'based on the words.'
  • 密奏以闻 - Means 'secretly presented for the emperor's knowledge,' where '密奏' (mi zou) means 'secretly presented' and '以闻' (yi wen) means 'for the emperor's knowledge.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 可汗 - Refers to the Khan, the leader of the Xianbei people, who was a powerful figure in the Northern Wei dynasty.
  • 惟一 - Refers to Weyi, a concubine or attendant in the palace.
  • 登 - Refers to Deng, a person who is likely a guard or attendant.
  • 后 - Refers to the Empress or a high-ranking lady in the palace.
  • 朱顶鹤 - Refers to Zhu Dinghe, a person who is likely a court official or a rival.
  • 清子 - Refers to Qingzi, a woman who is likely a servant or attendant.
  • 十香词 - Refers to a poem titled 'Ten Fragrant Words,' which is likely a love poem or a poem of praise.
  • 皇帝 - Refers to the Emperor, the ruler of the Northern Wei dynasty.
  • 至德统天 - Refers to the Emperor's virtue and authority, where '至德' (zhi de) means 'supreme virtue' and '统天' (tong tian) means 'to govern the heavens.'
  • 化及无外 - Means 'virtue extends to all corners,' where '化' (hua) means 'virtue' or 'influence' and '无外' (wu wai) means 'all corners.'
  • 寡妻匹妇 - Refers to widows and single women, often used in classical texts to denote the lowest social strata.
  • 宫帐深密 - Refers to the inner palace, which is often described as a place of secrecy and privacy.
  • 异言 - Refers to unusual or scandalous speech, which is often a source of concern in courtly settings.
  • 良非渺小 - Refers to the significance of the issue, emphasizing that it is not a trivial matter.
  • 隐讳 - Refers to the act of concealing information, which is often seen as a breach of duty in courtly settings.
  • 密奏以闻 - Refers to the act of secretly presenting information to the Emperor, which is often done to avoid public scandal or to maintain confidentiality.

[628]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 上览奏，大怒，即召后对诘 - The emperor read the memorial, became extremely angry, and immediately summoned the empress to question her.
  • 妾托体国家，已造妇人之极 - I, as a woman, have already reached the highest status in the state.
  • 况诞育储贰，近且生孙，儿女满前 - Moreover, I have given birth to the heir apparent, and recently, I have given birth to a grandson, with many children around me.
  • 此非汝作手书，更复何辞 - This is not your own handwriting, so what more can you say?
  • 此宋国忒里蹇所作 - This was written by Song Guo Te Li Jian.
  • 妾即从单登得而书赐之耳 - I received it from Dan Deng and wrote it as a gift.
  • 国家无亲蚕事，妾作那得有亲桑语 - Our state does not practice the ritual of the empress's own silkworm rearing, so how could I have written about it?
  • 诗正不妨以无为有 - The poem can easily fabricate something out of nothing.
  • 如词中合缝靴，亦非汝所着，为宋国服邪 - For example, the poem mentions a boot that fits perfectly, which is not something you wore, but it is for Song Guo.
  • 上怒甚，因以铁骨朵击后，后几至殒 - The emperor was extremely angry and struck the empress with an iron mace, nearly killing her.
  • 即下其事，使参知政事张孝杰与乙辛穷治之 - He immediately ordered the investigation of the matter, instructing Vice Chancellor Zhang Xiao Jie and Yixin to thoroughly investigate it.
  • 乙辛乃系械惟一、长命等讯鞫，加以钉灼荡错等刑，皆为诬服 - Yixin then had Wei Yi, Chang Ming, and others chained and interrogated, subjecting them to torture such as nail-piercing and scalding, and they all confessed under duress.
  • 狱成，将奏 - The case was completed, and they were about to report it.
  • 枢密副使萧惟信驰语乙辛、孝杰曰 - The Vice Minister of Military Affairs Xiao Wei Xin rushed to tell Yixin and Zhang Xiao Jie.
  • 懿德贤明端重，化行宫帐 - Her virtue, wisdom, and dignity are renowned, and her influence extends throughout the palace.
  • 且诞育储君，为国大本，此天下母也 - Moreover, she has given birth to the heir apparent, who is the foundation of the state; she is the mother of the world.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 上 - Refers to the emperor, the highest ruler in the state.
  • 后 - Refers to the empress, the highest-ranking woman in the imperial household.
  • 储贰 - Refers to the heir apparent, the designated successor to the throne.
  • 亲蚕事 - Refers to the ritual of the empress's own silkworm rearing, a symbolic act of agricultural and textile production.
  • 合缝靴 - Refers to a boot that fits perfectly, a metaphorical reference in the poem.
  • 铁骨朵 - Refers to an iron mace, a weapon used by the emperor to strike the empress.
  • 参知政事 - Refers to Vice Chancellor, a high-ranking official in the imperial government.
  • 枢密副使 - Refers to Vice Minister of Military Affairs, a high-ranking official in charge of military affairs.
  • 懿德贤明端重 - Refers to the empress's virtues of virtue, wisdom, and dignity.
  • 化行宫帐 - Refers to the empress's influence extending throughout the palace, indicating her widespread respect and authority.

Proper Nouns

  • 上 - The emperor.
  • 后 - The empress.
  • 储贰 - The heir apparent.
  • 亲蚕事 - The ritual of the empress's own silkworm rearing.
  • 合缝靴 - A boot that fits perfectly.
  • 铁骨朵 - An iron mace.
  • 参知政事 - Vice Chancellor.
  • 枢密副使 - Vice Minister of Military Affairs.
  • 懿德贤明端重 - The empress's virtues of virtue, wisdom, and dignity.
  • 化行宫帐 - The empress's influence extending throughout the palace.
  • 宋国忒里蹇 - The author of the poem.
  • 单登 - The person from whom the empress received the poem.
  • 张孝杰 - Vice Chancellor.
  • 乙辛 - A high-ranking official.
  • 萧惟信 - Vice Minister of Military Affairs.
  • 储君 - The heir apparent.
  • 天下母 - The mother of the world.

[629]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 而可以叛家仇婢一语动摇之乎？ - Rhetorical question challenging the credibility of a statement about rebelling against a household and hating a maidservant.
  • 公等身为大臣，方当烛照奸宄，洗雪冤诬，烹灭此辈，以报国家，以正国体。 - Call to action emphasizing the responsibilities of officials to expose evil, clear the innocent, and eliminate scoundrels to uphold national dignity.
  • 奈何欣然以为得其情也？ - Rhetorical question questioning the officials' judgment.
  • 此是皇后骂飞燕也，如何更作《十词》？ - Critique of the Empress's poetry, suggesting it is inappropriate.
  • 此正皇后怀赵惟一耳！ - Specific interpretation of the Empress's poetry.
  • 朕亲临天下，臣妾亿兆，而不能防闲一妇，更何施眉目腼然南面乎！ - Expression of frustration and self-criticism by the emperor.
  • 后乞更面可汗一言而死，不许。 - Description of the Empress's final request and the emperor's decision.
  • 后乃望帝所而拜，作绝命词曰： - Description of the Empress's final act and her composition of a farewell poem.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 叛家仇婢 - Common theme in Chinese literature and history referring to rebelling against a household and hating a maidservant.
  • 烛照奸宄 - Metaphor for exposing and eliminating evil-doers.
  • 洗雪冤诬 - Refers to clearing the names of the falsely accused.
  • 烹灭此辈 - Strong expression of eliminating scoundrels.
  • 飞燕 - Refers to Lady Feiyan, a famous concubine of Emperor Cheng of Han, often used as a symbol of beauty and jealousy.
  • 赵惟一 - A person, possibly a lover or close associate of the Empress.
  • 可汗 - Title for the emperor, often used in a more formal or respectful context.
  • 绝命词 - Farewell poem or last words, often composed by someone facing death.

Proper Nouns

  • 叛家仇婢 - Refers to rebelling against a household and hating a maidservant.
  • 烛照奸宄 - Metaphor for exposing and eliminating evil-doers.
  • 洗雪冤诬 - Refers to clearing the names of the falsely accused.
  • 烹灭此辈 - Strong expression of eliminating scoundrels.
  • 飞燕 - Refers to Lady Feiyan, a famous concubine of Emperor Cheng of Han.
  • 赵惟一 - A person, possibly a lover or close associate of the Empress.
  • 可汗 - Title for the emperor.
  • 绝命词 - Farewell poem or last words.

[630]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 嗟薄祐兮多幸 - A lament expressing a sense of being blessed despite one's lack of merit.
  • 羌作麗兮皇家 - Interjection expressing a sudden realization or emotion, meaning 'to be beautiful' or 'to adorn,' referring to being favored by the royal family.
  • 承昊穹兮下覆 - To receive the protection of the heavens, and 'to be covered or protected from below,' emphasizing dual protection.
  • 近日月兮分华 - To be close to the sun and moon, and 'to share in their brilliance,' suggesting being favored by celestial bodies.
  • 托后钧兮凝位 - To rely on the weight of the throne, and 'to stabilize one's position,' indicating a secure and honored place in the imperial hierarchy.
  • 忽前星兮启耀 - Suddenly, the star in front, and 'to shine brightly,' suggesting a sudden rise to prominence.
  • 虽衅累兮黄床 - Even though there are faults or sins, and '黄床' refers to the imperial bed or position, indicating awareness of one's shortcomings.
  • 庶无罪兮宗庙 - Perhaps without sin, and '宗庙' refers to the ancestral temples, suggesting a hope to avoid dishonor to the family lineage.
  • 欲贯鱼兮上进 - To want to follow the fish, a metaphor for aspiring to rise in rank, and '上进' means 'to advance upward.'
  • 乘阳德兮天飞 - To ride the virtue of the sun, and '天飞' means 'to fly to heaven,' suggesting a desire to achieve great heights.
  • 岂祸生兮无朕 - How could disaster arise, and '无朕' means 'without a sign,' indicating surprise or disbelief at sudden misfortune.
  • 蒙秽恶兮宫闱 - To be covered in filth and evil, and '宫闱' refers to the inner palace, suggesting a sense of being defiled or disgraced.
  • 将剖心兮自陈 - To be about to open one's heart, and '自陈' means 'to explain oneself,' indicating a willingness to confess or explain one's situation.
  • 冀回照兮白日 - To hope for a return of light, and '白日' means 'the bright sun,' suggesting a desire for clarity or justice to be restored.
  • 宁庶女兮多惭 - A humble woman, and '多惭' means 'to feel much shame,' indicating a sense of inadequacy or embarrassment.
  • 遏飞霜兮下击 - To stop the flying frost, and '下击' means 'to strike downward,' suggesting a desire to halt or counteract a negative force.
  • 顾子女兮哀顿 - To look at one's children, and '哀顿' means 'to be deeply saddened,' indicating sorrow for one's family.
  • 对左右兮衰伤 - To face those around, and '衰伤' means 'to be deeply grieved,' suggesting a sense of loss or grief among attendants or companions.
  • 共西曜兮将坠 - To share the setting sun, and '将坠' means 'to be about to fall,' indicating a sense of decline or impending doom.
  • 忽吾去兮椒房 - Suddenly, I leave, and '椒房' refers to the imperial harem, suggesting a sudden departure from the imperial court.
  • 呼天地兮惨悴 - To call upon heaven and earth, and '惨悴' means 'to be in a state of distress,' indicating profound sorrow or despair.
  • 恨今古兮安极 - To lament the present and past, and '安极' means 'to have no end,' suggesting endless regret or frustration.
  • 知吾生兮必死 - To know that I live, and '必死' means 'to be destined to die,' indicating an acceptance of mortality.
  • 又焉爱兮旦夕 - And why should I love, and '旦夕' means 'day and night,' suggesting a sense of futility or resignation in the face of impending death.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 昊穹 - Refers to the sky or heaven, often used in classical texts to denote the supreme power or the heavens.
  • 黄床 - Refers to the imperial bed or the imperial position, symbolizing the highest authority in the imperial court.
  • 宗庙 - Refers to the ancestral temples, important sites for rituals and ceremonies honoring ancestors.
  • 椒房 - Refers to the imperial harem, where the emperor's concubines and wives lived.
  • 西曜 - Refers to the setting sun, often used as a metaphor for the decline or end of something.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This opening line combines a lament and a realization, setting the tone for the entire text.
  • This line uses parallel structure to emphasize the dual protection and favor from both the heavens and the celestial bodies.
  • This line contrasts the stability of the imperial position with a sudden rise to prominence.
  • This line expresses a sense of guilt and a hope to avoid dishonor to the family lineage.
  • This line uses metaphors to express the desire for advancement and success.
  • This line expresses surprise and a sense of being defiled or disgraced.
  • This line expresses a willingness to confess and a hope for clarity or justice.
  • This line expresses a sense of inadequacy and a desire to counteract a negative force.
  • This line expresses sorrow for one's family and a sense of loss among one's attendants or companions.
  • This line expresses a sense of decline and a sudden departure from the imperial court.
  • This line expresses a profound sense of sorrow and endless regret.
  • This line expresses an acceptance of mortality and a sense of futility or resignation.

[631]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 遂闭宫以白练自经 - Literal description of the action: 'then closed the palace and hanged herself with white silk.'
  • 上怒犹未解 - Means 'the emperor's anger was still not appeased.'
  • 命裸后尸，以苇席裹还其家 - Means 'ordered her body to be stripped and wrapped in a reed mat and sent back to her family.'
  • 春秋三十有六 - Means 'she was thirty-six years old.'
  • 正符白练之语 - Means 'this exactly matched the prophecy of white silk.'
  • 闻者莫不冤之 - Means 'those who heard of it all felt she was wronged.'
  • 皇太子投地大呼 - Means 'the crown prince threw himself to the ground and shouted.'
  • 杀吾母者，耶律乙辛也 - Means 'the one who killed my mother is Yelü Yixin.'
  • 他日不门诛此贼，不为人子 - Means 'one day, if I do not exterminate this traitor, I am not a son.'
  • 乙辛遂谋害太子，无虚日矣 - Means 'Yelü Yixin then plotted to harm the crown prince without a single day's respite.'
  • 嗟嗟 - An expression of sorrow or lamentation.
  • 自古国家之祸，未尝不起于纤纤也 - Means 'since ancient times, the disasters of a state have never arisen without small beginnings.'
  • 固皆成于乙辛 - Means 'but they were all accomplished by Yelü Yixin.'
  • 此祸所由生也 - Means 'this is how the disaster originated.'
  • 第乙辛凶惨无匹 - Means 'Yelü Yixin was uniquely cruel and merciless.'
  • 而孝杰以儒业起家 - Means 'but Xiao Jie rose to prominence through his scholarly achievements.'
  • 必明于大义者 - Means 'he must have understood great principles.'
  • 使如维信直言，毅然诤之 - Means 'if he had spoken openly and firmly, as Wei Xin did.'
  • 后必不死 - Means 'the empress would not have died.'
  • 后不死，则太子可保无恙 - Means 'if the empress had not died, the crown prince could have been safe.'
  • 上亦何惭于少恩骨肉哉 - Means 'the emperor would have had no reason to feel ashamed of being unkind to his kin.'
  • 乃亦昧声同心，自保禄位 - Means 'but he too remained silent and self-serving, preserving his position.'
  • 卒使母后、储君与诸老成，一旦皆死于非辜 - Means 'he ultimately caused the empress, the crown prince, and the elderly officials to die unjustly.'
  • 此史册所书未有之祸也 - Means 'this is a disaster unprecedented in historical records.'
  • 二人者，可谓罪通天者乎 - Means 'can these two be considered guilty of crimes that reach to heaven?'
  • 懿德所以取祸者有三 - Means 'Empress Ede's three reasons for disaster.'
  • 曰好音乐与能诗、善书耳 - Means 'they are her love of music, her ability to compose poetry, and her skill in calligraphy.'
  • 假令不作《回心院》 - Means 'if she had not composed 'The Courtyard of Remembrance.'
  • 则《十香》词安得诬出后手乎 - Means 'how could the 'Ten Perfumes' poem have been falsely attributed to her?'
  • 至于《怀古》一诗 - Means 'as for the poem 'Pining for the Past.'
  • 则天实为之，而月食飞练，先命之矣 - Means 'it was truly ordained by heaven, and the moon eating the flying silk was a premonition.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 遂闭宫以白练自经 - Describes the act of suicide by hanging.
  • 上怒犹未解 - Refers to the emperor's ongoing anger.
  • 命裸后尸，以苇席裹还其家 - Describes the disrespectful treatment of the empress's body.
  • 春秋三十有六 - Refers to the empress's age at death.
  • 正符白练之语 - Refers to a prophecy about her death by hanging.
  • 闻者莫不冤之 - Expresses the widespread sympathy for the empress.
  • 皇太子投地大呼 - Describes the crown prince's reaction to his mother's death.
  • 杀吾母者，耶律乙辛也 - Identifies Yelü Yixin as the perpetrator.
  • 他日不门诛此贼，不为人子 - Expresses the crown prince's vow for revenge.
  • 乙辛遂谋害太子，无虚日矣 - Describes Yelü Yixin's ongoing plot against the crown prince.
  • 嗟嗟 - An expression of sorrow.
  • 自古国家之祸，未尝不起于纤纤也 - A general statement about the origins of state disasters.
  • 固皆成于乙辛 - Blames Yelü Yixin for the disasters.
  • 此祸所由生也 - Identifies the source of the disaster.
  • 第乙辛凶惨无匹 - Describes Yelü Yixin's cruelty.
  • 而孝杰以儒业起家 - Refers to Xiao Jie's rise to prominence through his scholarly achievements.
  • 必明于大义者 - Suggests that Xiao Jie should have understood moral principles.
  • 使如维信直言，毅然诤之 - Describes the ideal behavior of Wei Xin.
  • 后必不死 - Suggests that the empress would have survived if Xiao Jie had acted differently.
  • 后不死，则太子可保无恙 - Suggests that the crown prince would have been safe.
  • 上亦何惭于少恩骨肉哉 - Questions the emperor's lack of kindness to his kin.
  • 乃亦昧声同心，自保禄位 - Describes Xiao Jie's self-serving behavior.
  • 卒使母后、储君与诸老成，一旦皆死于非辜 - Describes the tragic deaths of the empress, the crown prince, and the elderly officials.
  • 此史册所书未有之祸也 - Describes the unprecedented nature of the disaster.
  • 二人者，可谓罪通天者乎 - Questions the severity of Xiao Jie and Wei Xin's crimes.
  • 懿德所以取祸者有三 - Lists the three reasons for Empress Ede's disaster.
  • 曰好音乐与能诗、善书耳 - Describes her interests and talents.
  • 假令不作《回心院》 - Refers to a specific poem.
  • 则《十香》词安得诬出后手乎 - Questions the attribution of a poem.
  • 至于《怀古》一诗 - Refers to another specific poem.
  • 则天实为之，而月食飞练，先命之矣 - Describes a celestial event as a premonition.

[632]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 敢实录其事 - Dared to record the events faithfully; indicates boldness in writing.
  • 一快观者 - Satisfies the reader's desire for justice; indicates the emotional impact of the events.
  • 一不了公案 - Refers to an unresolved public case or matter; indicates the ongoing nature of the events.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 姚叔祥 - Historical figure or scholar, likely a commentator or historian.
  • 鼎 - Historical figure or author, possibly a chronicler.
  • 乙辛 - Historical figure, likely a political or military leader.
  • 孝杰 - Historical figure, possibly a military or political leader.
  • 天祚 - Historical figure, likely an emperor or ruler.
  • 懿德皇后 - Empress Consort Yide, a historical figure.
  • 赵国公主 - Princess of Zhao, a historical figure.
  • 戴复古 - Poet and scholar from the Song Dynasty.
  • 江西 - Province in China, historically significant for its culture and literature.
  • 奁具 - Refers to dowry items, a cultural practice in traditional Chinese marriage.

Proper Nouns

  • 姚叔祥 - Historical figure or scholar.
  • 鼎 - Historical figure or author.
  • 乙辛 - Historical figure.
  • 孝杰 - Historical figure.
  • 天祚 - Historical figure.
  • 懿德皇后 - Empress Consort Yide.
  • 赵国公主 - Princess of Zhao.
  • 戴复古 - Poet and scholar.
  • 江西 - Province in China.
  • 奁具 - Dowry items.

[633]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 惜多才，怜薄命 - Expresses sorrow for the talent of the deceased and pity for their short life.
  • 无计可留汝 - Indicates that there is no way to keep the deceased, emphasizing the inevitability of death.
  • 揉碎花笺，忍写断肠句 - Metaphorically describes the act of writing a poignant letter, with '揉碎花笺' (crumpling the flowered paper) and '忍写断肠句' (writing heart-wrenching words) conveying deep emotional pain.
  • 道傍杨柳依依 - A classical allusion to the *Book of Songs* (Shijing), where '杨柳依依' (willows swaying gently) symbolizes parting and lingering affection.
  • 千丝万缕，抵不住一分愁绪 - Metaphorically compares the myriad threads of sorrow to the gentle swaying of willows, indicating that even the most delicate emotions cannot be contained.
  • 捉月盟言，不是梦中语 - Refers to a famous legend of the Tang poet Li Bai, who drowned while trying to catch the moon's reflection in the water. '捉月盟言' (oath to catch the moon) and '不是梦中语' (not words in a dream) suggest that the promise made was real and not a mere fantasy.
  • 后回君若重来，不相忘处，把杯酒浇奴坟土 - A vow to remember the deceased and pour a cup of wine on their grave if they return, expressing a deep and lasting commitment.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 戴之 - The name of the deceased.
  • 邓敞 - A historical figure, a poor scholar who failed the imperial examinations.
  • 牛奇章之子蔚 - The son of Niu Qizhang, who offered to help Deng Chang.
  • 李评事 - The name of the person Deng Chang was previously married to.
  • 牛氏 - The name of the woman Deng Chang later married.
  • 李氏 - The name of Deng Chang's first wife, Rui Shi.
  • 戴之无行，不待言矣 - Refers to the character of the deceased, suggesting that their behavior was not good, and this is self-evident.
  • 此妇性气，亦自可畏 - Describes the deceased's personality as formidable, indicating that she was strong-willed and possibly intimidating.
  • 昔邓敞以孤寒不第，牛奇章之子蔚谓敞曰 - Refers to a historical anecdote involving Deng Chang, a poor scholar who failed to pass the imperial examinations, and Niu Qizhang's son, who offered to help him marry his sister.
  • 吾父为宰相，兄弟皆在郎省 - Indicates that Niu Qizhang was a high-ranking official, and his sons held important positions in the government.
  • 纵不得富贵，岂无一嫁处邪 - Suggests that even if one does not achieve wealth or status, there are still opportunities for marriage.
  • 今愿一与夫人同之 - Expresses a desire to treat the other woman as an equal, despite the social hierarchy.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 惜多才，怜薄命，无计可留汝 - This sentence is a tripartite expression of sorrow, pity, and helplessness.
  • 揉碎花笺，忍写断肠句 - The parallel structure emphasizes the emotional intensity of the act of writing.
  • 道傍杨柳依依，千丝万缕，抵不住一分愁绪 - The sequence of images builds up to the central theme of overwhelming sorrow.
  • 捉月盟言，不是梦中语 - The contrast between the reality of the oath and the dream-like quality of the promise adds depth to the emotional content.
  • 后回君若重来，不相忘处，把杯酒浇奴坟土 - The conditional structure and the act of pouring wine on the grave create a powerful image of remembrance and commitment.

  • 盖亦妇墓，而其夫为文志之 - It seems to be a wife's tomb, and her husband wrote a text to record it.
  • 仁卿尚能记其略 - 尤仁卿 still remembered the general outline.
  • 云：“某里人卢孝妻，祝氏月英。” - Said, 'A person from a certain village, the wife of Lu Xiao, surname Zhu, name Yueying.'
  • 父某，母某 - Father and mother, placeholders for unknown names.
  • 始娉其姊，姊为权力者夺去 - Initially betrothed to her sister, but the sister was taken away by a powerful person.
  • 父母以英续盟 - Parents arranged for Yueying to continue the alliance.
  • 英貌庄性慧 - Yueying had a dignified appearance and a wise nature.
  • 事舅颇极礼敬 - Served her father-in-law with great respect.
  • 女工、经、史、音乐，皆能精晓 - Skilled in women's work, classics, history, and music.
  • 日不废书，夜必刺缉 - Did not neglect books during the day and always embroidered and sewed at night.
  • 夫妇唱随，未尝离舍 - Husband and wife sang and followed each other, never leaving the house.
  • 偶患脾泻 - Once suffered from diarrhea.
  • 而前势力者复欲谋夺英 - The former powerful person again wanted to plot to take Yueying.
  • 鹰犬之客，平起风波 - Hired thugs calmly stirred up trouble.
  • 英愤恚火郁暴死 - Yueying, furious and suppressed, died suddenly.
  • 归孝三年，年二十一岁 - Returned to her husband after three years, aged twenty-one.
  • 惊魂两飞，不知离合 - Shocked spirit flew apart, not knowing if they were separated or together.
  • 死不知生，生何以知死 - Death does not know life, how can life know death.
  • 尽力营葬，恨无再遇之期 - Did everything possible to arrange the burial, regretting no more meeting time.
  • 血泪如麻，不能止息 - Tears of blood like hemp, could not stop.
  • 散衣十九件，皆英手刺花鸟 - Scattered nineteen pieces of clothing, all embroidered by Yueying's hand.
  • 人谓画工不如 - People say painters are not as good as her.
  • 并其生平玩好，悉以归冥 - And her lifelong hobbies, all returned to the netherworld.
  • 至正二年某月日，夫卢孝撰 - Written by her husband Lu Xiao on a certain day of the second year of the Zhizheng era.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 熙宁末 - End of the Xining era, a period in the reign of Emperor Shenzong of Song (1068-1085).
  • 洛中 - Means 'in Luoyang,' referring to the city of Luoyang during the Song Dynasty.
  • 耕于凤凰山下 - Literally means 'plowing under the Phoenix Mountain,' indicating the location where the discovery was made.
  • 获石碣 - Means 'found a stone stele,' referring to the discovery of an inscribed stone monument.
  • 方广一尺余 - Describes the size of the stone stele as being more than one chi (a traditional Chinese unit of length) square.
  • 乃妇人撰夫志铭 - Means 'then a woman wrote her husband's epitaph,' indicating the authorship and subject of the inscription.
  • 君姓曹氏，名禋，字礼夫 - Provides the personal details of the deceased, including his surname (曹), given name (禋), and courtesy name (礼夫).
  • 世为洛阳人 - Indicates that the deceased's family had been residents of Luoyang for generations.
  • 三十岁，两举不第，卒于长安道中 - Describes the deceased's age, his failure in two examinations, and his death on a road in Chang'an (the capital city during the Tang Dynasty).
  • 朝廷卿大夫、乡里故老闻之，莫不哀其‘孝友睦姻，笃行能文，何其夭之如是邪！’ - Expresses the widespread sorrow and lamentation over the deceased's early death, highlighting his virtues.
  • 唯儿闻之独不然，乃慰其母曰 - Means 'only the son heard it and did not react the same way, instead comforting his mother.'
  • 家有南亩，足以养其亲；室有遗文，足以教其子 - Indicates the son's reassurance to his mother about the family's resources and his father's legacy.
  • 凡累乎阴阳之间者，至死数不可逃，夫何悲喜之有哉 - Philosophical reflection on the inevitability of fate and the futility of emotional attachment to life and death.
  • 丙子年三月十八日卒，以其年十月十五日葬于凤凰山之原 - Provides the exact dates of the deceased's death and burial.
  • 余姓周氏，君妻也，归曹已八载矣 - Introduces the author of the epitaph, a woman surnamed Zhou, who had been married to the deceased for eight years.
  • 生子一人，尚幼 - Indicates that the deceased left behind one young son.
  • 以其恩义之不可忘，故作铭 - Explains the reason for writing the epitaph, emphasizing the importance of remembering the deceased's virtues.
  • 铭曰 - Introduces the inscription, which follows in the form of a poem.
  • 其生也天，其死也天，苟达此理，哀哉何言 - Reflects on the idea that both life and death are determined by fate, and thus, mourning is unnecessary.
  • 其生也浮，其死也休，终何为哉，慰母之忧 - Concludes the inscription by suggesting that the deceased's life was transient, and his death brought peace, ultimately serving to comfort his mother's grief.

  • 读卢孝文，所遭厄甚矣，虽欲达其将能乎 - '读卢孝文' means 'reading Lu Xiaowen,' '所遭厄甚矣' means 'suffered great misfortune,' '虽欲达其将能乎' means 'even if she wanted to express her thoughts, could she do so?'

  • 已而必贵谢世 - This phrase reflects the traditional Chinese belief in the cycle of fortune and misfortune, where wealth and status are often temporary.

  • 萧然茅店 - A thatched inn is a common setting in classical Chinese literature, often used to depict a humble or impoverished lifestyle.
  • 卖酒路旁 - Selling wine by the roadside is a common occupation for those in poverty, reflecting the economic hardships faced by many.
  • 虽患难之中 - This phrase emphasizes the resilience of individuals even in difficult circumstances.
  • 琼奴无从昔时容态 - This describes a change in appearance, often used to highlight the passage of time and the effects of hardship.
  • 青年粉质，终异常人 - Despite her current state, Qiong Nu still retains a youthful and extraordinary beauty, which is a common theme in classical Chinese literature.
  • 有吴指挥者悦之 - Wu Commander is a military official, and his attraction to Qiong Nu reflects the social dynamics of the time.
  • 欲娶为妾 - The practice of polygamy, particularly the taking of concubines, was common among the elite in ancient China.
  • 童氏以既聘辞 - The refusal based on the fact that Qiong Nu was already betrothed reflects the social norms and expectations of the time.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 信难裁 - Ambiguous phrase; could mean 'it is difficult to make a decision' or 'it is hard to control' depending on context.
  • 金钗脱股，宝镜离台 - Metaphors for separation and loss; '金钗脱股' refers to a golden hairpin falling from its holder, and '宝镜离台' refers to a precious mirror being removed from its stand, both symbolizing the separation of lovers.
  • 万里辽阳 - Metaphor for a long distance, often used to describe the separation of lovers.
  • 郎去也，甚日重回 - '郎' is a term of endearment for a man, often used by a woman to address her lover. '甚日重回' means 'when will you return?' expressing longing and uncertainty.
  • 丁香树，含花到死，肯傍别人开 - Metaphorical statement; '丁香树' (lilac tree) symbolizes a woman who remains faithful to her lover. '含花到死' means 'holding its flowers until death,' and '肯傍别人开' means 'would it bloom for someone else?' The whole phrase expresses unwavering loyalty and fidelity.
  • 自缢于房中 - Means 'hanged herself in the room,' indicating a tragic act of suicide.
  • 母觉而救解 - Means 'the mother noticed and saved her,' showing the intervention of the mother to prevent the suicide.
  • 吴指挥者闻之怒 - Means 'the commander Wu heard of it and was angry,' indicating the reaction of a high-ranking official.
  • 使麾下碎其酿器，逐去他居 - Means 'ordered his subordinates to smash her brewing equipment and drive her away to another place,' showing the harsh punishment meted out to the woman.
  • 欲折困之 - Means 'intended to break her spirit,' indicating the purpose behind the punishment.
  • 时有老驿使杜君 - Means 'there was an old postmaster named Du,' introducing a character who plays a significant role in the story.
  • 亦常山人 - Means 'also from Changshan,' providing additional information about the character.
  • 必贵存日，相与善 - Means 'when Bìguì was alive, they were good friends,' indicating a past relationship between the characters.
  • 怜童氏孤苦 - Means 'pitied Tóng's loneliness and hardship,' showing the character's compassion.
  • 假以驿廊一间而安焉 - Means 'gave her a room in the post station to stay,' indicating the character's kindness.
  • 一日，客有戎服者三四人投驿中 - Means 'one day, a few guests dressed in military uniforms arrived at the post station,' introducing new characters and setting the stage for the next part of the story.
  • 杜君问所从来，其人曰 - Means 'Du asked where they came from, and the people replied,' indicating the interaction between the characters.
  • 吾侪辽东某卫总小旗，差往海南取军，此假宿耳 - Means 'We are from a certain guard unit in Liaodong, sent to Hainan to recruit soldiers, and we are staying here for the night,' providing background information about the guests.
  • 值童氏偶出帘下 - Means 'Tóng happened to come out from behind the curtain,' describing a chance encounter.
  • 中一少年，特淳谨，不类武卒 - Means 'one of the young men was especially sincere and cautious, unlike a typical soldier,' highlighting the character's unusual demeanor.
  • 数往还相视，而凄惨之色可掬 - Means 'they looked at each other several times, and the sad expression was evident,' showing the emotional connection between the characters.
  • 童氏心动，因出问之 - Means 'Tóng was moved and went out to ask him,' indicating her curiosity and emotional response.
  • 对曰 - Means 'he replied,' starting the dialogue.
  • 苕姓徐，浙江常山人 - Means 'My name is Xu Tiáo, from Changshan, Zhejiang,' providing the character's name and origin.
  • 幼时，父尝聘同里沈必贵女 - Means 'When I was young, my father once betrothed me to a girl from the same village, Shěn Bìguì's daughter,' providing background information about the character's past.
  • 未婚，而两家坐事谪戍，不相闻者数年矣 - Means 'We were not married, but both families were exiled to military service, and we lost contact for several years,' explaining the separation.
  • 适因入室，见妈妈状貌，酷与苕外母相类，故不觉感怆，非有他也 - Means 'Just now, when I entered the room, I saw your mother's appearance, which was very similar to my late mother-in-law, so I couldn't help but feel sad, it's nothing else,' explaining the emotional reaction.
  • 童氏复问 - Means 'Tóng asked again,' continuing the conversation.
  • 沈家今在何处？厥女何名 - Means 'Where is Shěn's family now? What is the name of his daughter?' asking for more information.
  • 曰 - Means 'he replied,' continuing the dialogue.
  • 女名琼奴，字润贞 - Means 'Her name is Qióngnú, and her courtesy name is Rùnzhen,' providing the name of the girl.
  • 联亲时年方十四，以今计之，当十九矣 - Means 'When we were betrothed, she was fourteen years old. By now, she should be nineteen,' providing the age of the girl.
  • 第知戍海南，忘其所寓州郡，难已寻觅 - Means 'But I only know that she is stationed in Hainan, and I have forgotten the state or prefecture where she lives, making it difficult to find her,' expressing the difficulty in locating her.

  • 泪为生别滋 - Means 'tears flow from the pain of separation,' emphasizing the emotional impact of parting.
  • 努力爱春华 - Means 'strive to cherish the spring blossoms,' encouraging the enjoyment of life's fleeting moments.
  • 莫忘欢乐时 - Means 'do not forget the times of joy,' reminding the wife to remember happy moments.
  • 生当复来归 - Means 'if I live, I will return,' expressing hope for a future reunion.
  • 死当长相思 - Means 'if I die, I will always think of you,' expressing eternal love even in death.
  • 足下胤子无恙 - Means 'your son is well,' addressing Ruan Ling and assuring him of the safety of his child.
  • 天或者启其情 - Means 'perhaps heaven has opened its heart,' suggesting divine intervention or fate.
  • 以延忠臣之世 - Means 'to extend the legacy of loyal ministers,' expressing the hope for continued loyalty and honor.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 苏子卿 - Refers to Su Wu, a famous Han dynasty diplomat and envoy to the Xiongnu.
  • 匈奴 - Refers to the Xiongnu, a nomadic people who were often at odds with the Han dynasty.
  • 李陵 - Refers to Li Ling, a Han general who defected to the Xiongnu and wrote letters to Su Wu.
  • 燕王旦 - Refers to Yan Wang Dan, a Han prince who was involved in a rebellion against the Han court.
  • 元 - Refers to Su Wu's son, who was involved in a rebellion and was executed.
  • 胡妇子 - Refers to the son of Su Wu and a Xiongnu woman, who was later adopted as Su Wu's heir.
  • 王道 - Refers to the way of kingship, a concept in Confucianism emphasizing moral governance.
  • 人情 - Refers to human feelings and emotions, a central theme in Confucian ethics.

  • 喜乐籍一小鬟 - He took pleasure in registering or acquiring a young maid; '喜乐籍' implies indulgence, and '一小鬟' refers to a young maid or servant girl.
  • 未几召还 - Not long after, he was summoned back to the capital; '未几' means 'not long after,' and '召还' means 'to be summoned back.'
  • 作诗寄后政 - He composed a poem to send to his successor; '作诗' means 'to compose a poem,' and '寄后政' means 'to send to the successor.'
  • 管领回 - To be entrusted to the care of the east wind; '管领' means 'to take care of,' and '回' means 'to return' or 'to bring back.'
  • 以胭脂寄其人 - He sent lipstick to the person; '以' means 'to send,' '胭脂' refers to lipstick or rouge, and '寄其人' means 'to send to the person.'
  • 纷纷坠叶飘香砌 - Leaves fall纷纷 and fragrance wafts through the courtyard; '纷纷' means 'falling纷纷ly,' '坠叶' means 'fallen leaves,' '飘' means 'waft,' and '香砌' refers to 'fragrant courtyard.'
  • 珍珠帘卷玉楼空 - The pearl curtains are rolled up, and the jade tower is empty; '珍珠帘' refers to 'pearl curtains,' '卷' means 'rolled up,' '玉楼' means 'jade tower,' and '空' means 'empty.'
  • 愁肠已断无由醉 - My sorrow has broken my heart, and I cannot drink to forget; '愁肠' means 'sorrowful heart,' '已断' means 'broken,' '无由' means 'no way,' and '醉' means 'to drink to forget.'
  • 残灯明灭枕头欹 - The dimming lamp flickers, and the pillow is tilted; '残灯' means 'dimming lamp,' '明灭' means 'flickering,' and '枕头欹' means 'tilted pillow.'
  • 谙尽孤眠滋味 - I have experienced the taste of solitary sleep; '谙尽' means 'experienced fully,' '孤眠' means 'solitary sleep,' and '滋味' means 'taste' or 'experience.'
  • 都来此事 - All this matter; '都来' means 'all this,' and '此事' means 'this matter.'
  • 眉间心上 - Between the brows and in the heart; '眉间' means 'between the brows,' and '心上' means 'in the heart.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 厉声自呼曰 - He loudly called out to himself, saying; '厉声' means 'loudly' or 'sharply,' and '自呼' means 'called out to himself.'
  • 张悦其人 - Zhang was pleased with the person; '悦' means 'pleased' or 'delighted,' and '其人' means 'the person.'
  • 政以能制，见其不能忘 - The governance was effective because he could control himself, but he saw that he could not forget; '政以能制' means 'governance was effective because he could control himself,' and '见其不能忘' means 'he saw that he could not forget.'
  • 赵阅道、张乖崖，皆能制其情者 - Zhao Yuedao and Zhang Guayi, both of whom could control their emotions; '皆能制其情者' means 'both of whom could control their emotions.'
  • 于席上赠官妓小英歌曰 - At a banquet, he gifted an official courtesan named Xiao Ying with a song, saying; '于席上' means 'at a banquet,' '赠' means 'gifted,' '官妓' means 'official courtesan,' and '小英歌' means 'a song for Xiao Ying.'
  • 天教搏（抟）百媚，相映明如花 - Heaven taught her to possess a hundred graces, which shine as brightly as flowers; '天教' means 'Heaven taught,' '搏（抟）百媚' means 'possess a hundred graces,' and '相映明如花' means 'shine as brightly as flowers.'
  • 往近桃花坊正（北）面，门庭掩映如仙家 - She lived near the Peach Blossom Lane, with her door and courtyard hidden like those of a fairy; '往近' means 'lived near,' '桃花坊' means 'Peach Blossom Lane,' '正（北）面' means 'north side,' and '门庭掩映如仙家' means 'her door and courtyard hidden like those of a fairy.'
  • 美人宜称言不得，龙脑换（薰）衣香入骨 - A beautiful woman is not to be spoken of lightly, her clothes fragrant with dragon brain incense; '美人宜称言不得' means 'a beautiful woman is not to be spoken of lightly,' and '龙脑换（薰）衣香入骨' means 'her clothes fragrant with dragon brain incense.'
  • 维扬软縠如云英，亳郡轻纱似蝉翼 - The soft silk from Weiyang is as light as clouds, and the light gauze from Bozhou is as thin as cicada wings; '维扬软縠' means 'soft silk from Weiyang,' '如云英' means 'as light as clouds,' '亳郡轻纱' means 'light gauze from Bozhou,' and '似蝉翼' means 'as thin as cicada wings.'
  • 我疑天上婺女星之精，偷入筵中名小英 - I suspect that she is the essence of the Weaving Maid Star, who stole into the banquet and was named Xiao Ying; '我疑' means 'I suspect,' '天上婺女星之精' means 'the essence of the Weaving Maid Star,' '偷入筵中' means 'stole into the banquet,' and '名小英' means 'was named Xiao Ying.'
  • 又疑王母侍儿初失意，谪向人间为歌妓 - I also suspect that she is a fairy maid from the Queen Mother of the West who lost favor and was exiled to the mortal world as a singing girl; '又疑' means 'I also suspect,' '王母侍儿' means 'fairy maid from the Queen Mother of the West,' '初失意' means 'lost favor,' '谪向人间' means 'exiled to the mortal world,' and '为歌妓' means 'as a singing girl.'
  • 不然何得肤如红玉初碾成，眼似秋波双脸横 - Otherwise, how could she have skin as red as newly polished jade and eyes like autumn waves across her face? '不然何得' means 'otherwise, how could she have,' '肤如红玉初碾成' means 'skin as red as newly polished jade,' and '眼似秋波双脸横' means 'eyes like autumn waves across her face.'
  • 舞态因风欲飞去，歌声遏云长且清 - Her dancing posture, swayed by the wind, seemed to want to fly away, and her singing voice stopped the clouds, long and clear; '舞态因风欲飞去' means 'her dancing posture, swayed by the wind, seemed to want to fly away,' and '歌声遏云长且清' means 'her singing voice stopped the clouds, long and clear.'
  • 有时歌罢下香砌，几人魂魄遥相惊 - Sometimes, after she finished singing, she stepped down from the fragrant steps, and several people's souls were startled from afar; '有时歌罢' means 'sometimes, after she finished singing,' '下香砌' means 'stepped down from the fragrant steps,' and '几人魂魄遥相惊' means 'several people's souls were startled from afar.'

  • 人看小英心已足，我见小英心未足 - Contrasts the satisfaction of others with the speaker's dissatisfaction, suggesting a deeper emotional or personal connection.

  • 在蜀娶婢，三年后归其父，犹然完璧 - Narrative describing a man who married a servant girl in Sichuan, returned her to her father after three years, and she remained intact, implying fidelity or purity.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 风惊绿 - Poetic expression referring to a green object (like leaves or grass) being startled or disturbed by the wind, conveying a sense of movement or agitation.
  • 若待得君来向此 - Conditional phrase meaning 'if you were to come here,' using '若' (if) and '得' (to obtain or arrive) to create a hypothetical scenario.
  • 花前对酒不忍触 - Poetic phrase meaning 'unable to bear touching the flowers while drinking wine,' conveying a sense of delicate emotion or hesitation.
  • 共粉泪，雨簌簌 - Metaphorical expression where '粉泪' (powder tears) refers to tears mixed with makeup, and '雨簌簌' (rain dripping) describes the sound of tears falling, evoking a scene of weeping.
  • 米元章有洁癖 - Straightforward statement that 'Mi Yuanzhang has a compulsion for cleanliness,' using the modern term '洁癖' (compulsion for cleanliness) for clarity.
  • 或言其矫 - Phrase meaning 'some say it is feigned' or 'some claim it is exaggerated,' suggesting that the cleanliness is not entirely genuine.
  • 家多声伎 - Phrase meaning 'has many musicians and performers,' referring to entertainers often in a household setting.
  • 使数卒解衣袒臂，奉其酒馔 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'had several soldiers undress and bare their arms to serve the wine and food,' emphasizing the soldiers' service.
  • 姬侍环于他客，杯盘狼藉 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'the concubines surrounded the other guests, and the cups and plates were in disarray,' conveying a chaotic scene.
  • 久之，亦自迁坐于众宾之间 - Phrase meaning 'after a while, he also moved to sit among the other guests,' indicating a passage of time.
  • 相传有洁癖者，米元章、倪元镇二人 - Phrase meaning 'it is said that two people with a compulsion for cleanliness are Mi Yuanzhang and Ni Yuancheng,' indicating a traditional belief.
  • 元镇于女色，少所当意 - Phrase meaning 'Ni Yuancheng had few women who pleased him,' suggesting a lack of satisfaction.
  • 俾之浴 - Phrase meaning 'had her bathe,' indicating a command or request.
  • 复俾浴 - Phrase meaning 'had her bathe again,' emphasizing the action.
  • 东方既白，不复作巫山之梦 - Phrase meaning 'when the east began to turn white, he no longer dreamed of the mountains of Wu,' referring to the early morning and a metaphor for sexual dreams.
  • 情主人曰 - Phrase meaning 'the master of emotions said,' using a metaphorical title for someone who is deeply emotional.
  • 元章之癖，不胜其情 - Phrase meaning 'Mi Yuanzhang's compulsion could not withstand his emotions,' suggesting that his compulsion was overwhelmed by his feelings.
  • 元镇之情，不胜其癖 - Phrase meaning 'Ni Yuancheng's emotions could not withstand his compulsion,' suggesting that his emotions were overwhelmed by his compulsion.
  • 且其不能忘情则一也 - Phrase meaning 'and their inability to forget their emotions is the same,' suggesting a shared emotional attachment.
  • 王昆之回面，避妓也 - Phrase meaning 'Wang Kun turned away, avoiding the courtesan,' describing a physical action.
  • 陈烈之逾墙，逃妓也 - Phrase meaning 'Chen Lie climbed over the wall, escaping the courtesan,' describing a physical action.
  • 杨忠襄之焚衣，誓妓也 - Phrase meaning 'Yang Zhongxiang burned his clothes, vowing to avoid the courtesan,' describing a symbolic action.
  • 徵仲之弄臭脚，果以求脱妓也 - Phrase meaning 'Zhengzhong played with his smelly feet, indeed seeking to escape the courtesan,' describing a humorous and exaggerated physical action.
  • 是皆情之至者，诚虑忽不自制，故预违之 - Phrase meaning 'all of these are examples of extreme emotion, truly fearing a sudden loss of self-control, so they acted in advance to avoid it,' suggesting a deep emotional response.
  • 鲁男子之情，十倍于柳下惠 - Phrase meaning 'the emotion of the man from Lu is ten times that of Liu Xiaohui,' using historical figures to suggest a strong emotional contrast.
  • 伊川之强制，万不若明道先生 - Phrase meaning 'the forced restraint of Yichuan is not even a thousandth of Mingdao Master,' using historical figures to suggest a strong contrast in emotional control.

  • 數月，左股內隱隱聞嬰兒啼聲 - After several months, he heard faint baby cries inside his left thigh, a description of the monk's miraculous conception.

  • 埋却袈裟离世网 - Literally means 'burying the robe to escape the world's web,' a metaphor for renouncing worldly affairs and becoming a monk.
  • 寄生二子在人家 - Means 'leaving two sons in someone's house,' suggesting that the monk left his children with others before becoming a monk.
  • 神通骑出斑斑虎 - Refers to a supernatural ability to ride a tiger, a common motif in Buddhist and Taoist stories symbolizing the control over fear and danger.
  • 感应呼来白白蛇 - Means 'calling a white snake through感应 (感应),' which suggests a spiritual connection or divine intervention.
  • 是圣是凡君莫测 - Means 'whether he is a saint or an ordinary person, you cannot tell,' indicating the monk's mysterious and possibly supernatural qualities.
  • 相逢休笑亦休夸 - Means 'when you meet him, do not laugh or boast,' suggesting a humble and respectful attitude towards the monk.

  • 接垒桥通何处路，倚栏人是阿谁家 - A line from Zhiye's poem, describing a bridge connecting unknown paths and a person leaning on a railing, whose home is unknown. This line reflects the monk's poetic and philosophical nature.
  • 圣保寺 - Refers to Shengbao Temple, a temple where Zhiye lived and practiced.

  • 桃花生子菜花台（薹），细雨蛙声出草莱 - A line from the poem, describing the growth of peach blossoms and rapeseed flowers, and the sound of frogs in the grass, evoking a peaceful and natural scene.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 西廂 - Refers to *The Western Chamber* (*西廂記*), a classic Chinese play by Wang Shifu, which tells the story of Zhang Sheng and Cui Yingying.

  • 自投於瀨水 - Refers to the act of throwing oneself into the Lishui River, a tragic act of suicide.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 子猶曰 - Conventional phrase meaning 'Zi You said,' introducing a quotation from the text *Zi You*.
  • 同一識英雄俊眼 - Ambiguous phrase; could mean 'to recognize heroes with keen eyes' or 'to have the same insight as heroes.'
  • 幸則為紅拂妓，雄服連辔 - Phrase using the story of Hong Fu to describe a fortunate outcome; '紅拂妓' refers to Hong Fu, a legendary courtesan who became the wife of Li Yuanji.
  • 不幸則為擊绵女，寒风濑水 - Phrase using a contrasting story to describe a tragic fate; '擊绵女' symbolizes a tragic and isolated fate.
  • 或言此女可以无死，甚不然也 - Phrase translating to 'some say this woman can avoid death, but that is very wrong.'
  • 田光先生有云 - Conventional phrase meaning 'Mr. Tian Guang said,' introducing a quotation from Mr. Tian Guang.
  • 长者为行，不使人疑 - Phrase translating to 'a virtuous person acts in a way that does not cause suspicion.'
  • 掩夫人之壶浆，勿令其露 - Phrase translating to 'cover the lady's wine and food, do not let it be seen.'
  • 此女不死，子胥虽行，终未释然也 - Phrase translating to 'if this woman does not die, even if Sun Bin goes, he will not be at ease.'
  • 知礼义之不可越亏，而犹然跪进壶浆，劝勉加餐 - Phrase translating to 'knowing that propriety and righteousness cannot be violated, yet still kneeling and offering wine and food, encouraging and urging to eat more.'
  • 独念子胥非恒人故耳 - Phrase translating to 'only thinking that Sun Bin is not an ordinary person.'
  • 既知其非恒人，亦何惜一死，以安其魂，而定其事乎 - Phrase translating to 'if one knows that he is not an ordinary person, why not sacrifice one's life to rest his soul and settle the matter.'
  • 此女虽终身不嫁，冥冥之中，固已嫁子胥矣 - Phrase translating to 'even if this woman never marries in this life, in the afterlife, she has already married Sun Bin.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 或云曼之它子 - Suggests an alternative view of their parentage, indicating they might be the other sons of Man.
  • 与阳阿主家令赵临共里巷，托附临，屡为组文刺绣献临，临愧受之 - Describes their relationship with Zhao Lin, the chief of the Yang'e palace, and the gifts they gave him.
  • 居临家，称临女 - Indicates that they lived with Zhao Lin and were referred to as his daughters.

  • 且专事膏沐澡粉，其费亡所爱 - Indicates their lavish spending on personal grooming and cosmetics.

  • 飞燕贫，与合德共被 - Indicates their close relationship despite their poverty, sharing a blanket.
  • 夜雪，期射鸟者于舍旁，飞燕露立，闭息顺气，体温舒，亡疹粟，射鸟者异之，以为神仙 - Describes a magical or supernatural moment where Zhao Feiyan's presence amazes the guard, who thinks she is a fairy.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 羽林 - Imperial Guards, a military unit responsible for the protection of the emperor.
  • 射鸟者 - A guard skilled in shooting birds, indicating a position of trust and skill.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The exact meaning of '膏滑' in '合德膏滑，出浴不濡' can be ambiguous, possibly meaning 'oily and slippery' or 'smooth and slippery.'
  • The phrase '流转至长安' in '飞燕姊弟流转至长安' can be read as 'were exiled to Chang'an' or 'were transported to Chang'an,' depending on the context.
  • The phrase '人称赵主子' in '于时人称赵主子' can be interpreted as 'people called them the sons of Zhao' or 'people referred to them as the children of Zhao.'
  • The long sequence in '飞燕姊弟事阳阿主家为舍直，常窃效歌舞，积思精切，听至终日，不得食' describes their activities and dedication, but the exact division of actions and their sequence can be ambiguous.
  • The sequence in '夜雪，期射鸟者于舍旁，飞燕露立，闭息顺气，体温舒，亡疹粟，射鸟者异之，以为神仙' is a vivid description of a magical moment, but the exact sequence of events and the guard's reaction can be ambiguous.

  • 合德 - Zhao Hede
  • 阳阿主 - Yang'e palace
  • 羽林 - Imperial Guards

  • 庐江灊人 - Indicates the consort's origin from the area of Qian in Lujiang.
  • 抚军将军 - A high-ranking military position.
  • 戢女 - The consort's full name.
  • 南郡王妃 - The title of a consort for the Prince of Nanjun.
  • 文惠太子 - A title referring to the son of Emperor Wu of Liu Song, known as Emperor Ming of Liu Song.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 如伉俪 - Means 'like a couple' or 'like husband and wife.'
  • 流涕覆面 - Describes the act of crying and covering one's face.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 女巫 - Refers to a female shaman, a person who practices divination or magic.
  • 太庙 - The ancestral temple where the emperor and his family pay respects to their ancestors.
  • 武帝曜云殿 - Refers to the Yao Yun Hall, a palace building named after Emperor Wu.

Proper Nouns

  • 女巫子杨珉之 - A female shaman named Yang Minzhi.
  • 太孙 - The crown prince, who later became emperor.
  • 高昌县都乡君 - A title of nobility given to the empress's legitimate mother Liu.
  • 余杭广昌乡君 - A title of nobility given to the empress's biological mother Song.
  • 明帝 - Emperor Ming.
  • 阿奴 - A term of endearment used by the emperor to address the empress.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 半面妆 - Half-face makeup, a type of makeup where only half of the face is painted.
  • 洪醉 - Drunk heavily.
  • 手加刀刃 - Cut with a knife.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 梁元帝 - Emperor Yuan of Liang, a ruler of the Southern Dynasties period.
  • 徐妃 - Xu Zhaopei, the wife of Emperor Yuan of Liang.
  • 天监十六年十二月 - December of the sixteenth year of Emperor Wu of Liang's Tianjian era.
  • 湘东王妃 - The title of the wife of Prince Xiangdong.
  • 世子方等 - The eldest son of Emperor Yuan of Liang.
  • 益昌公主含贞 - A princess named Hanzhen.
  • 荆州后堂瑶光寺 - The Yuguang Temple in the rear garden of Jingzhou.
  • 智远道人 - A monk named Zhiduan.
  • 暨季江 - A person named Ji Jijiang.
  • 贺徽 - A person named He Hui.
  • 普贤尼寺 - The Puxian Nunnery.
  • 贞惠世子方诸母王氏 - The mother of the second son of Emperor Yuan of Liang.
  • 太清三年 - The third year of Emperor Yuan of Liang's Taichang era.
  • 江陵瓦官寺 - The Wanguan Temple in Jiangling.
  • 《金楼子》 - A historical text written by Emperor Yuan of Liang.

Proper Nouns

  • 梁元帝 - Emperor Yuan of Liang.

[711]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 僧徒遥指太后，以弄昙献 - Monks pointed to the empress from afar, toying with Tanxian. '遥指' (pointing from afar) and '弄' (toying with) describe a manipulative action.
  • 太上者 - The Supreme One, a title often given to emperors or high-ranking officials.
  • 帝闻太后不谨，而未之信 - The emperor heard that the empress was not chaste, but did not believe it. '不谨' (not chaste) is a euphemism for infidelity.
  • 二少尼 - Two young nuns, where '少尼' refers to young female Buddhist monks.
  • 悦而召之，乃男子也 - He was pleased and summoned them, only to find they were men. '悦' (pleased) and '召之' (summoned them) are straightforward, but the revelation that they were men adds a twist.
  • 伏法 - To be punished by law, a euphemism for execution.
  • 元山王三郡君，皆太后所昵也 - The Prince of Yuanshan, the Three Marquisates, and the Three Counties, all close to the empress. '所昵' (close to) indicates a personal relationship.
  • 帝视晋阳，奉太后还邺 - The emperor looked to Jinyang and escorted the empress back to Ye. '视' (looked to) and '奉' (escorted) are formal terms.
  • 紫陌 - Purple Road, a poetic name for a road, often associated with imperial travel.
  • 舍人魏僧伽明风角 - The attendant Wei Sengga was skilled in divination. '风角' (divination) refers to a specific form of fortune-telling.
  • 即时当有暴逆事 - There will be a sudden and violent uprising. '暴逆' (violent uprising) is a serious accusation.
  • 弯弓缠矟驰入城 - He drew his bow, wrapped his spear, and galloped into the city. '弯弓' (drew his bow) and '缠矟' (wrapped his spear) are vivid descriptions of preparation for action.
  • 幽太后北宫 - Imprisoned the empress in the Northern Palace. '幽' (imprisoned) is a strong term.
  • 敕内外诸亲，一不得与太后相见 - Ordered all relatives inside and outside the palace not to see the empress. '敕' (ordered) is a formal command.
  • 周使元伟来聘 - The Zhou envoy Yuan Wei came to pay a visit. '聘' (visit) can also mean to pay respects.
  • 作《述行赋》 - Wrote the 'Fu on the Journey'. '述行' (journey) and '赋' (fu) are literary terms.
  • 郑庄公克段而迁姜氏 - Duke Zhuang of Zheng defeated his brother Duan and exiled Jiangshi. This is a reference to a historical event from the *Zuo Zhuan*.
  • 文虽不工，当时深以为愧 - Although the writing was not refined, it deeply shamed the contemporaries. '不工' (not refined) and '深以为愧' (deeply ashamed) are evaluative phrases.
  • 齐亡入周，恣行奸秽 - After the fall of Qi, he entered Zhou and acted shamelessly. '恣行' (acted shamelessly) and '奸秽' (shameful acts) are strong terms.
  • 隋开皇中殂 - He died in the Kaihuang era of the Sui dynasty. '殂' (died) is a formal term.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 僧徒 - Monks, referring to Buddhist monks.
  • 太后 - Empress, a high-ranking female figure in the imperial court.
  • 昙献 - A historical figure, possibly a monk or a court official.
  • 太上者 - A title often given to emperors or high-ranking officials.
  • 晋阳 - Jinyang, an ancient city in Shanxi province.
  • 邺 - Ye, an ancient capital of the Cao Wei kingdom.
  • 紫陌 - Purple Road, a poetic name for a road, often associated with imperial travel.
  • 舍人魏僧伽 - Attendant Wei Sengga, a historical figure known for his skills in divination.
  • 风角 - A form of divination.
  • 暴逆 - Violent uprising, a serious political threat.
  • 北宫 - Northern Palace, a specific palace in the imperial complex.
  • 周使元伟 - Zhou envoy Yuan Wei, a historical figure who visited the court.
  • 《述行赋》 - Fu on the Journey, a literary work.
  • 郑庄公克段而迁姜氏 - A historical event from the *Zuo Zhuan*.
  • 齐亡入周 - Refers to the fall of the Qi state and the subsequent political changes.
  • 隋开皇中 - The Kaihuang era of the Sui dynasty.

[712]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 逼通 - Euphemism for sexual coercion; indicates that Empress Hu forced herself upon Yang Baihua.
  • 追思 - To remember or grieve for the past; describes Empress Hu's ongoing grief.
  • 连臂蹋足 - Traditional dance where people hold hands and stomp their feet; describes the performance of the song by palace women.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 魏灵太后 - Empress Hu, a powerful figure in Northern Wei history, known for her political acumen and controversial policies.
  • 杨白花 - Historical figure whose story is often cited in Chinese literature and folklore; his flight to Liang and the subsequent song by Empress Hu are part of this narrative.
  • 梁 - The Southern Dynasty, a rival state to Northern Wei, known for its cultural and literary achievements.
  • 杨白花歌 - Song composed by Empress Hu in memory of Yang Baihua; a historical example of how literature and music were used to express personal and political sentiments in ancient China.

Proper Nouns

  • 魏灵太后 - Empress Hu, the regent during the reign of Emperor Xiaoming of Northern Wei.
  • 武都 - A region in modern-day Gansu and Sichuan, known for its historical significance.
  • 杨白花 - Yang Baihua, a historical figure whose story is often cited in Chinese literature and folklore.
  • 梁 - The Southern Dynasty, a rival state to Northern Wei.
  • 杨白花歌 - The song composed by Empress Hu in memory of Yang Baihua.

[714]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 上恚曰 - The emperor was angry and said, where '恚' (hui) means anger or resentment.
  • 畜生何足付大事 - What kind of beast is fit for great matters? An expression of contempt, suggesting that the person being referred to is unworthy of important responsibilities.
  • 独孤诚误我 - Dugu Cheng has misled me, where '误我' (wu wo) means to mislead or deceive the speaker.
  • 召我儿 - Summon my son, a direct command to bring the emperor's son.
  • 勇也 - It's Yong, referring to the name of the emperor's son, Yang Yong.
  • 书讫 - After writing it down, indicating the completion of a written order.
  • 示左仆射杨素 - Show it to the Left Minister Yang Su, where '示' (shi) means to show or display, and '左仆射' (zuoyushu) is the title of a high-ranking official.
  • 太子遣张衡入寝殿 - The crown prince sent Zhang Heng into the sleeping chamber, where '遣' (qian) means to send, and '寝殿' (qindian) refers to the emperor's private quarters.
  • 遂令夫人及后宫同侍疾者，并出就别宫 - And ordered the empress and the consorts who were tending to the emperor's illness to leave and go to another palace, where '遂' (sui) means then or subsequently, and '别宫' (biegong) refers to a different palace.
  • 俄闻上崩 - Soon after, they heard that the emperor had died, where '俄' (e) means soon, and '崩' (beng) is the euphemism for the death of an emperor.
  • 夫人与诸后宫相顾曰 - The empress and the consorts looked at each other and said, where '相顾' (xianggu) means to look at each other.
  • 得免死矣 - We have been spared from death, expressing relief.
  • 陈氏恚而却坐 - Lady Chen was angry and sat back, where '恚' (hui) means anger, and '却坐' (quezuo) means to sit back or retreat.
  • 不肯致谢诣 - Refused to go and thank him, where '致谢' (zhixie) means to express gratitude, and '诣' (yi) means to go to or visit.
  • 其夜，太子烝焉 - That night, the crown prince had relations with her, where '烝' (zheng) is a euphemism for sexual intercourse, often used in historical texts to avoid explicit language.
  • 炀帝即位之后 - After Emperor Yang ascended the throne, where '炀帝' (yangdi) is the posthumous name of Emperor Yang of Sui.
  • 出居先都宫 - Was sent out to live in the former capital palace, where '先都宫' (xiandugong) refers to the old imperial palace.
  • 寻召入 - Later, he was summoned back, where '寻' (xun) means soon or shortly.
  • 岁余而终 - After more than a year, she died, where '岁余' (suoyu) means more than a year.
  • 帝深悼之，为制《伤神赋》 - The emperor deeply mourned her and composed the 'Grief of the Spirit' ode, where '悼之' (diao zhi) means to mourn, and '伤神赋' (shangshenfu) is the title of the ode.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 上 - Refers to the emperor, specifically Emperor Wen of Sui.
  • 独孤诚 - A high-ranking official, likely a trusted advisor to the emperor.
  • 太子 - Refers to the crown prince, Yang Yong, who later became Emperor Wen of Sui.
  • 炀帝 - The posthumous name of Emperor Yang of Sui, the second emperor of the Sui dynasty.
  • 伤神赋 - A literary work composed by Emperor Yang of Sui to mourn the death of Yang Yong's mother.

Proper Nouns

  • 上 - The emperor, Emperor Wen of Sui.
  • 独孤诚 - A high-ranking official, likely a trusted advisor to the emperor.
  • 太子 - The crown prince, Yang Yong.
  • 炀帝 - The posthumous name of Emperor Yang of Sui.
  • 伤神赋 - A literary work composed by Emperor Yang of Sui to mourn the death of Yang Yong's mother.

[715]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 得幸于太宗为才人 - Literally means 'was favored by Emperor Taizong as a concubine,' indicating a relationship between Emperor Taizong and Wu Zetian.
  • 赐号武媚 - Means 'was given the name Wu Mei,' where '赐号' means 'given a title' and '武媚' is the name given to her.
  • 入侍太宗疾 - Means 'entered to serve Emperor Taizong when he was ill,' indicating her role as a caretaker.
  • 遂即东厢烝焉 - Means 'and they immediately engaged in intimate relations in the eastern chamber,' where '烝' is a euphemism for sexual intercourse.
  • 太宗崩 - Means 'Emperor Taizong died,' marking the end of his reign.
  • 武氏为尼 - Means 'Wu Zetian became a nun,' indicating her status after Emperor Taizong's death.
  • 忌日 - Means 'the anniversary of his death,' referring to the day when Emperor Taizong's death is commemorated.
  • 上诣寺行香 - Means 'the emperor visited the temple to burn incense,' indicating a religious ritual.
  • 武氏见上而泪 - Means 'Wu Zetian saw the emperor and wept,' indicating her emotional reaction.
  • 王后疾萧淑妃之宠 - Means 'Empress Wang was jealous of Consort Xiao's favor,' indicating the conflict between Empress Wang and Consort Xiao.
  • 阴令武氏长发，纳之后宫 - Means 'she secretly ordered Wu Zetian to grow her hair and admit her into the imperial harem,' indicating a strategic move by Empress Wang.
  • 武氏巧慧多权术 - Means 'Wu Zetian was clever and skilled in manipulation,' indicating her character traits.
  • 屈体事后 - Means 'she humbled herself to serve Empress Wang,' indicating her behavior towards Empress Wang.
  • 后数称其美 - Means 'Empress Wang often praised her beauty,' indicating the positive reception of Wu Zetian.
  • 未几大幸 - Means 'shortly after, she was greatly favored,' indicating the rise in Wu Zetian's status.
  • 拜为昭仪 - Means 'she was appointed as a high-ranking concubine,' indicating her official position.
  • 后及淑妃宠皆衰 - Means 'the favor of Empress Wang and Consort Xiao declined,' indicating the decline in their status.
  • 更相与谮之 - Means 'they alternately slandered her,' indicating the ongoing conflict and slander.
  • 上皆不纳 - Means 'the emperor did not accept their accusations,' indicating the emperor's stance.
  • 及武后生子 - Means 'when Wu Zetian gave birth to a son,' indicating a significant event.
  • 上欲废后而立之 - Means 'the emperor wanted to depose Empress Wang and replace her with Wu Zetian,' indicating the emperor's intention.
  • 褚遂良谏曰 - Means 'Zhu Suilian advised, saying,' indicating the advice given by Zhu Suilian.
  • 武氏经事先帝 - Means 'Wu Zetian has served the late emperor,' indicating her past service.
  • 天下耳目，安可蔽也 - Means 'the eyes and ears of the world cannot be deceived,' indicating the public scrutiny.
  • 万代之后，谓陛下为何如主 - Means 'in future generations, how will people speak of Your Majesty,' indicating the long-term consequences.
  • 武氏在帘中大言曰 - Means 'Wu Zetian loudly said from behind the curtain,' indicating her reaction.
  • 何不扑杀此獠 - Means 'why not beat this barbarian to death,' indicating her harsh words.
  • 上乃逐遂良，而立武氏 - Means 'the emperor then dismissed Zhu Suilian and established Wu Zetian as empress,' indicating the final decision.
  • 王皇后与萧妃并废 - Means 'Empress Wang and Consort Xiao were both deposed,' indicating the outcome of the conflict.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 唐高宗武后 - Refers to Wu Zetian, the empress consort and later empress regnant of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 太宗 - Refers to Emperor Taizong of Tang, the second emperor of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 高宗 - Refers to Emperor Gaozong of Tang, the third emperor of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 太子 - Refers to the crown prince, the heir apparent to the throne.
  • 昭仪 - A high-ranking concubine in the imperial harem.
  • 褚遂良 - A prominent official and historian of the Tang Dynasty, known for his loyalty and integrity.
  • 王后 - Refers to Empress Wang, the first wife of Emperor Gaozong.
  • 萧淑妃 - Refers to Consort Xiao, a favorite concubine of Emperor Gaozong.
  • 忌日 - The anniversary of the death of a person, often marked by mourning rituals.
  • 行香 - A ritual involving burning incense in temples or shrines.
  • 阴令 - Secretly ordering or instructing.
  • 权术 - Strategies or tactics used to gain power or influence.
  • 屈体 - Humbling oneself or showing deference.
  • 谮 - Slandering or defaming someone.
  • 扑杀 - Beating to death.
  • 獠 - A derogatory term for a barbarian or outsider.
  • 逐 - Dismissing or expelling from office.
  • 并废 - Deposing or removing from power simultaneously.

Proper Nouns

  • 唐高宗 - Emperor Gaozong of Tang.
  • 武后 - Wu Zetian.
  • 太宗 - Emperor Taizong of Tang.
  • 高宗 - Emperor Gaozong of Tang.
  • 太子 - The crown prince.
  • 昭仪 - A high-ranking concubine.
  • 褚遂良 - A prominent official and historian.
  • 王后 - Empress Wang.
  • 萧淑妃 - Consort Xiao.
  • 忌日 - The anniversary of Emperor Taizong's death.

[717]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 敏之少韶秀 - Describes Li Shimin as youthful, handsome, and charming.
  • 輕俊自喜 - Indicates that he was proud of his own elegance and grace.
  • 與私通 - Refers to an illicit relationship.
  • 俾繼士彟 - Means 'to make him the heir of Li Shiji' (Li Shiji was Li Shimin's father).
  • 逼淫之 - Indicates forced sexual intercourse.
  • 固辭 - Means to firmly refuse or decline.
  • 乾没自用 - Refers to embezzlement or misappropriation of funds.
  • 褫衰粗 - Means to remove mourning clothes.
  • 奏音樂 - Refers to playing music.
  • 悉逼亂之 - Indicates that he forced and corrupted all the palace attendants who came to visit his mother.
  • 流雷州 - Means to be exiled to Leizhou.
  • 表復故姓 - Refers to a petition to restore his original surname.
  • 道中自經死 - Means that he hanged himself along the way.
  • 奉士彟后 - Means to be adopted as the heir of Li Shiji.
  • 進號天后 - Means to be advanced to the title of Empress.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 敏之 - Refers to Li Shimin, a member of the Tang imperial family.
  • 楊氏 - Refers to Yang Guifei's grandmother, who had an illicit relationship with Li Shimin.
  • 士彟 - Refers to Li Shiji, the father of Li Shimin.
  • 太平公主 - Refers to Princess Taiping, a powerful figure in the Tang dynasty.
  • 上元元年 - Refers to the first year of the Shangyuan era (760 CE).

Proper Nouns

  • 敏之 - Li Shimin
  • 楊氏 - Yang Guifei's grandmother
  • 士彟 - Li Shiji
  • 后 - Wu Zetian (Empress Wu)
  • 太平公主 - Princess Taiping
  • 司卫少卿杨思俭女 - Yang Guifei
  • 元爽子承嗣 - Li Yuanxiang
  • 上元元年 - The first year of the Shangyuan era (760 CE)

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 義陽宣城公主 - Princesses Yiyang and Xuancheng, daughters of Empress Xiao, who were favored by Empress Wu.
  • 幽掖庭幾四十不嫁 - The princesses were confined in the palace for nearly forty years without being married, reflecting the political intrigue and power struggles of the time.
  • 太子弘言於帝 - Prince Hong, the eldest son of Emperor Gaozong, who was a potential heir.
  • 後怒，鴆殺弘 - Empress Wu's ruthless elimination of potential rivals, including her own son.
  • 帝將下詔遜位於後 - Emperor Gaozong's intention to abdicate in favor of Empress Wu, indicating her growing influence.
  • 宰相郝處俊固諫 - Hao Chujun, a loyal minister who opposed Empress Wu's ambitions.
  • 帝病頭，眩不能視 - Emperor Gaozong's illness, which provided an opportunity for Empress Wu to gain more power.
  • 侍醫張文仲、秦鳴鶴曰 - Court physicians who were trusted by the emperor and had the authority to treat him.
  • 風上逆，砭血，頭可愈 - A medical treatment method involving bloodletting to cure headaches.
  • 後內幸帝疾得自專 - Empress Wu's secret hope that the emperor's illness would allow her to take full control of the government.
  • 醫議疾，烏可罪 - The defense of the doctors, emphasizing the importance of medical advice.
  • 醫一再刺 - The doctors' actions, which were successful in curing the emperor's illness.
  • 吾目明矣 - The emperor's recovery, which was seen as a divine sign.
  • 天賜我師 - Empress Wu's interpretation of the doctors' success as a divine favor.
  • 身負繒寶以賜 - Her generous reward to the doctors, showing her gratitude and power.
  • 帝崩，中宗即位，天後稱皇太后 - The succession of Emperor Zhongzong and Empress Wu's elevation to Empress Dowager.
  • 遺詔軍國大務聽參決 - The will that granted Empress Wu significant political power.
  • 嗣聖元年，太后廢帝為盧陵王 - Empress Wu's deposal of Emperor Zhongzong, marking the beginning of her rule.
  • 自臨朝，以睿宗即帝位 - Her takeover of the government and the installation of Emperor Ruizong.
  • 後坐武成殿，帝率群臣上號冊 - The formal ceremony where Empress Wu was honored.
  • 越三日，太后臨軒冊帝 - The formal coronation of Emperor Ruizong.
  • 自是太后常御紫宸殿，施參紫帳臨朝 - Empress Wu's regular court sessions, symbolizing her supreme authority.
  • 尊考為太師 - Her honoring of her father, reflecting her political strategy.
  • 魏三妣為王妃 - Her honoring of her mothers-in-law, further consolidating her power.

  • 乃杀牛取血画大像，首高二百丈，云怀义刺膝血为之 - Describes a gruesome method of creating a large Buddha statue using the blood of a sacrificed cow, with the statue's head being 200 feet tall. The phrase '怀义刺膝血' (Huaiyi's knee-blood) is particularly unusual and may refer to a specific individual or event, but its exact meaning is unclear.
  • 张像于天津桥南，设斋 - '张像' means to display or set up the statue, and '设斋' refers to holding a vegetarian meal or ritual offering.

  • 是夕，密烧天堂，延及明堂，火照城中如昼，比明皆尽 - Describes a secret fire set at night that spread from the '天堂' (Tiantang, meaning 'Heavenly Hall') to the '明堂' (Mingtang, meaning 'Bright Hall'), illuminating the city like day and consuming everything by morning.
  • 暴风裂血像为数百段 - A violent storm shattered the blood-stained statue into hundreds of pieces.
  • 太后耻而讳之，但云内作工徒，误烧麻主，遂涉明堂 - The Empress Dowager was ashamed and tried to cover up the incident, claiming it was an accident caused by workers burning a '麻主' (má zhǔ), which could refer to a type of fabric or material. The phrase '遂涉明堂' suggests that the fire spread to the Bright Hall.
  • 命更造之，仍以怀义充使 - The Empress Dowager ordered the statue to be rebuilt and appointed Huaiyi to oversee the project.
  • 又铸铜为九州鼎及十二神，皆高一丈，各置其方 - This describes the casting of bronze statues representing the nine provinces and twelve deities, each standing one zhang (approximately 3.3 meters) tall, placed in their respective locations.

  • 宫禁事重，伏愿不令异姓出入 - Means 'the palace is a place of great importance, I humbly request that no outsiders be allowed to enter.'
  • 太后曰：“德卿厚意” - Means 'the Empress Dowager said, 'Deqing has good intentions.'
  • 易之、昌宗见太后疾笃，恐祸及己，引用党援，阴为之备 - Means 'Zhang Yizhi and Changzong, seeing the Empress Dowager's condition was serious, feared they would be implicated and began to gather allies and prepare secretly.'
  • 屡有人为飞书，及榜其书于通衢云：“易之兄弟谋反” - Means 'many times people wrote anonymous letters, and posted them in public places saying, 'The brothers Zhang Yizhi are plotting a rebellion.'
  • 太后皆不问 - Means 'the Empress Dowager did not inquire into these matters.'
  • 明年正月，赦天下，改元 - Means 'in the following year's first month, a general amnesty was declared, and the era name was changed.'
  • 太后疾益甚，惟二张居中用事 - Means 'the Empress Dowager's condition worsened further, and only the two Zhangs held power.'
  • 宰相张柬之等定计，率飞骑五百人，至东宫，迎皇太子至玄武门，斩关而入，诛昌宗、易之于庑下 - Means 'Prime Minister Zhang Jianzhi and others devised a plan, leading five hundred elite cavalry to the Eastern Palace, where they escorted the Crown Prince to Xuanwu Gate, broke through the gates, and killed Changzong and Yizhi in the portico.'
  • 是日，悉诛张昌期等 - Means 'on that day, all the Zhangs were executed.'
  • 太后传位太子 - Means 'the Empress Dowager abdicated the throne to the Crown Prince.'
  • 韦后居中宫 - Means 'Empress Wei resided in the central palace.'
  • 上官昭容与政事 - Means 'Shangguan Zhaorong participated in state affairs.'
  • 敬晖等将尽诛诸武 - Means 'Jing Hui and others intended to exterminate all the Wu clan.'
  • 武三思惧，乃因昭容入请，得幸于后，卒谋晖等诛之 - Means 'Wu Sansi, fearing for his life, used Shangguan Zhaorong to gain favor with the Empress, and eventually plotted to have Jing Hui and others killed.'
  • 初帝幽废，与后约：“一朝见天日，不相制” - Means 'when the emperor was first imprisoned and deposed, he made an agreement with the Empress: 'If one day I see the light of day again, I will not control you.'
  • 至是，与三思叩御床博戏，帝从旁典筹，不为忤 - Means 'by then, he was playing dice with Wu Sansi, and the emperor, sitting beside them, did not object.'
  • 三思讽群臣上后号为顺天皇后 - Means 'Wu Sansi persuaded the ministers to give the Empress the title 'Empress of Heaven.'
  • 乃亲谒宗庙，赠父玄贞上洛郡王 - Means 'she then personally paid homage to the ancestral temples and posthumously honored her father Xuanzhen as the Prince of Shangluo.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 海陵嬖寵愈多 - 海陵 refers to Emperor Wu of Jin (Wu Zetian), who had many favorites. 嬖寵 means favored concubines or mistresses.
  • 定哥希得見 - 希 means rarely, and 得見 means to be seen or to have an audience. Together, it means that Ding Gao rarely got to see the emperor.
  • 號呼求去，诅骂海陵 - 號呼 means to shout or cry out, and 求去 means to request departure. 诅骂 means to curse or abuse. Together, it means that Ding Gao shouted, requested to leave, and cursed Emperor Wu.
  • 海陵阳为不闻而去 - 阳为不闻 means to pretend not to hear, and 而去 means to leave. Together, it means that Emperor Wu pretended not to hear and left.
  • 与家奴阎乞儿通 - 通 means to have a relationship or connection, often implying an illicit one. Here, it means that Ding Gao had an affair with a slave named Yan Qier.
  • 尝以衣服遗乞儿 - 尝 means to give or bestow, and 衣服 means clothes. Together, it means that Ding Gao often gave clothes to Yan Qier.
  • 给事本位 - 给事 means to serve or work, and 本位 means one's own position or place. Together, it means that Yan Qier served in his own position within the palace.
  • 欲复与乞儿通 - 欲复 means to want to again, and 通 means to have a relationship or connection. Together, it means that Ding Gao wanted to resume her affair with Yan Qier.
  • 使比丘尼向乞儿索所遗衣服以调之 - 使 means to send, 比丘尼 means Buddhist nun, 向 means to go to, 索 means to ask for, 所遗 means the given, and 调之 means to tempt or seduce. Together, it means that Ding Gao sent a Buddhist nun to Yan Qier to ask for the clothes she had given him and to tempt him.
  • 笑曰：“妃今日富贵忘我耶！ - 笑曰 means to laugh and say, 妃 means empress, 今日 means today, 富贵 means wealth and honor, and 忘我耶 means have you forgotten me? Together, it means that Yan Qier laughed and said, 'Empress, have you forgotten me today because of your wealth and honor?'
  • 使人诘责阍者曰：“我天子妃，亲体之衣，尔故元视何也？我且奏之！ - 使人诘责 means to send someone to reprimand, 阍者 means gatekeeper, 我天子妃 means I am the empress, 亲体之衣 means my personal clothing, 尔故元视何也 means why did you not check, and 我且奏之 means I will report this. Together, it means that Ding Gao sent someone to reprimand the gatekeeper, saying, 'I am the empress, my personal clothing, why did you not check? I will report this.'
  • 阍者惶恐曰：“死罪。请后不敢！ - 惶恐 means to be terrified, 死罪 means a grave mistake, and 请后不敢 means please do not do it again. Together, it means that the gatekeeper was terrified and said, 'A grave mistake. Please do not do it again.'
  • 使人以箧盛乞儿，载入宫中 - 使人以箧盛 means to send someone to put in a box, 乞儿 means Yan Qier, and 载入宫中 means to bring into the palace. Together, it means that Ding Gao sent someone to put Yan Qier in a box and bring him into the palace.
  • 阍者果不敢复索 - 果 means indeed, and 不敢复索 means did not dare to search again. Together, it means that the gatekeeper indeed did not dare to search again.
  • 使衣妇人衣，杂诸宫婢，抵暮遣出 - 使衣妇人衣 means to dress in women's clothes, 杂诸宫婢 means among the palace maids, and 抵暮遣出 means until dusk and send out. Together, it means that Yan Qier was dressed in women's clothes, mixed among the palace maids, and sent out until dusk.
  • 贵哥以告海陵 - 贵哥 is another name for Ding Gao, 以告 means to report, and 海陵 means Emperor Wu. Together, it means that Guiguo reported this to Emperor Wu.
  • 定哥缢死 - 缢死 means to die by hanging. Together, it means that Ding Gao died by hanging.
  • 乞儿及比丘尼三人皆伏诛 - 伏诛 means to be executed. Together, it means that Yan Qier and the three Buddhist nuns were all executed.
  • 封贵哥萃莘国夫人 - 封 means to bestow, 贵哥 is another name for Ding Gao, and 萃莘国夫人 means Lady of Cuisen State. Together, it means that Guiguo was bestowed the title of Lady of Cuisen State.
